title: Maximum sum of non-adjacent elements (DP 5)
content:
Problem Statement: Given an array of N positive integers, we need to return the maximum sum of the subsequence such that no two elements of the subsequence are adjacent elements in the array.

Note: A subsequence of an array is a list with elements of the array where some elements are deleted (or not deleted at all) and the elements should be in the same order in the subsequence as in the array.

Examples 
Input: nums = [1, 2, 4]
Output: 5
Explanation: 
Subsequence {1,4} gives maximum sum.


Input:  [2, 1, 4, 9]
Output: 11
Explanation: 
Subsequence {2,9} gives maximum sum

Disclaimer: Here is the practice link to help you assess your knowledge better. It's highly recommend trying to solve it before looking at the solution.

Memoization
Algorithm
As we need to find the sum of subsequences, one approach that comes to our mind is to generate all subsequences and pick the one with the maximum sum.

To generate all the subsequences, we can use the pick/non-pick technique. This technique can be briefly explained as follows:
  • At every index of the array, we have two options.
  • First, to pick the array element at that index and consider it in our subsequence.
  • Second, to leave the array element at that index and not to consider it in our subsequence.

We can construct the recursive relation as follows:
  • At any index, pick the current element but then skip the next one
  • Or you can skip the current element and try from the next one
If we implement this recursive logic directly, it will recompute the same subproblems multiple times, especially for large inputs. To avoid recomputation, we use memoization i.e. we store the answer for each index once computed. This way if we need the solution for index i again, we just look it up in the memo table instead of recalculating.

Code
1234567891011121314151617181920212223242526272829303132333435363738394041424344454647
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;

class Solution {
public:
    // Recursive function with memoization
    int solve(vector<int>& arr, int i, vector<int>& dp) {
        // If index is negative, no element to pick
        if (i < 0) return 0;

        // If at first element, return its value
        if (i == 0) return arr[0];

        // Return already computed value
        if (dp[i] != -1) return dp[i];

        // Include current and move 2 back
        int pick = arr[i] + solve(arr, i - 2, dp);

        // Exclude current and move 1 back
        int notPick = solve(arr, i - 1, dp);

        // Store and return max of both choices
        return dp[i] = max(pick, notPick);
    }

    // Main function to be called externally
    int maximumNonAdjacentSum(vector<int>& arr) {
        int n = arr.size();

        // DP array initialized with -1
        vector<int> dp(n, -1);

        // Start solving from last index
        return solve(arr, n - 1, dp);
    }
};

int main() {
    vector<int> arr = {2, 1, 4, 9};
    Solution obj;

    // Output the result
    cout << obj.maximumNonAdjacentSum(arr);
    return 0;
}
123456789101112131415161718192021222324252627282930313233343536373839404142434445
class Solution {
    // Recursive helper function with memoization
    public int solve(int[] arr, int i, int[] dp) {
        // No element to pick
        if (i < 0) return 0;

        // Only one element
        if (i == 0) return arr[0];

        // Return memoized result
        if (dp[i] != -1) return dp[i];

        // Pick current and move 2 back
        int pick = arr[i] + solve(arr, i - 2, dp);

        // Don't pick current, move 1 back
        int notPick = solve(arr, i - 1, dp);

        // Memoize and return result
        return dp[i] = Math.max(pick, notPick);
    }

    // Main function to compute result
    public int maximumNonAdjacentSum(int[] arr) {
        int n = arr.length;

        // Initialize DP array
        int[] dp = new int[n];
        Arrays.fill(dp, -1);

        // Start recursion
        return solve(arr, n - 1, dp);
    }
}

public class Main {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        int[] arr = {2, 1, 4, 9};
        Solution obj = new Solution();

        // Print result
        System.out.println(obj.maximumNonAdjacentSum(arr));
    }
}
12345678910111213141516171819202122232425262728293031323334353637383940
class Solution:
    # Recursive helper with memoization
    def solve(self, arr, i, dp):
        # No elements to pick
        if i < 0:
            return 0

        # One element case
        if i == 0:
            return arr[0]

        # Return memoized result
        if dp[i] != -1:
            return dp[i]

        # Pick current and go 2 steps back
        pick = arr[i] + self.solve(arr, i - 2, dp)

        # Skip current and go 1 step back
        notPick = self.solve(arr, i - 1, dp)

        # Store and return max
        dp[i] = max(pick, notPick)
        return dp[i]

    # Main function to call
    def maximumNonAdjacentSum(self, arr):
        n = len(arr)

        # Initialize DP array
        dp = [-1] * n

        # Start recursion
        return self.solve(arr, n - 1, dp)

# Driver code
arr = [2, 1, 4, 9]
obj = Solution()
print(obj.maximumNonAdjacentSum(arr))
12345678910111213141516171819202122232425262728293031323334353637383940
class Solution {
    // Recursive helper with memoization
    solve(arr, i, dp) {
        // No element to pick
        if (i < 0) return 0;

        // Only one element
        if (i === 0) return arr[0];

        // Return memoized value
        if (dp[i] !== -1) return dp[i];

        // Pick current and go 2 back
        let pick = arr[i] + this.solve(arr, i - 2, dp);

        // Skip current and go 1 back
        let notPick = this.solve(arr, i - 1, dp);

        // Memoize and return
        dp[i] = Math.max(pick, notPick);
        return dp[i];
    }

    // Main function to start computation
    maximumNonAdjacentSum(arr) {
        let n = arr.length;

        // Initialize DP array
        let dp = new Array(n).fill(-1);

        // Start solving
        return this.solve(arr, n - 1, dp);
    }
}

// Driver code
let arr = [2, 1, 4, 9];
let obj = new Solution();
console.log(obj.maximumNonAdjacentSum(arr));
Complexity Analysis Time Complexity: O(N), where N = total no. of elements in array. The overlapping subproblems will return the answer in constant time O(1).
Space Complexity: O(N+N), extra space used for memoization and auxiliary stack space.
Tabulation
Algorithm
To prevent auxiliary stack space used by recursion in memoization, and to optimize space and runtime, we use the tabulation (bottom-up DP) approach.
  • Create a DP array to store the maximum sum at each index.
  • Initialize the first value in the DP array as the first element of the input array because if there's only one element, that’s the only sum we can take.
  • For the second element, take the maximum between the first and the second element.
  • From the third element onward, for each index, calculate two options:
    • If we pick the current element, we add its value to the value at index two positions back in the DP array
    • If we don’t pick, just take the value from the previous index.
  • Take the maximum of the two options and store it in the current position in the DP array.
  • After processing all elements, the last value in the DP array will contain the maximum sum possible without picking adjacent elements.

Code
12345678910111213141516171819202122232425262728293031323334353637383940
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;

class Solution {
public:
    // Function to return maximum sum of non-adjacent elements
    int maximumNonAdjacentSum(vector<int>& arr) {
        // Get the size of array
        int n = arr.size();

        // If array has only one element, return it
        if (n == 1) return arr[0];

        // Initialize dp array of size n
        vector<int> dp(n);

        // Base case: only one element
        dp[0] = arr[0];

        // Base case: max of first or second element
        dp[1] = max(arr[0], arr[1]);

        // Iterate from 3rd element onwards
        for (int i = 2; i < n; i++) {
            // Either take current and add dp[i-2]
            // Or skip current and take dp[i-1]
            dp[i] = max(arr[i] + dp[i - 2], dp[i - 1]);
        }

        // Final result at last index
        return dp[n - 1];
    }
};

int main() {
    vector<int> arr = {2, 1, 4, 9};
    Solution obj;
    cout << obj.maximumNonAdjacentSum(arr);
    return 0;
}

class Solution {
    // Function to return maximum sum of non-adjacent elements
    public int maximumNonAdjacentSum(int[] arr) {
        // Get the size of array
        int n = arr.length;

        // If only one element, return it
        if (n == 1) return arr[0];

        // Initialize dp array
        int[] dp = new int[n];

        // Base case
        dp[0] = arr[0];

        // Compare first two elements
        dp[1] = Math.max(arr[0], arr[1]);

        // Iterate from index 2
        for (int i = 2; i < n; i++) {
            // Take current and dp[i-2] or just dp[i-1]
            dp[i] = Math.max(arr[i] + dp[i - 2], dp[i - 1]);
        }

        // Return result from last index
        return dp[n - 1];
    }
}

public class Main {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        Solution sol = new Solution();
        int[] arr = {2, 1, 4, 9};
        System.out.println(sol.maximumNonAdjacentSum(arr));
    }
}

class Solution:
    # Function to return maximum sum of non-adjacent elements
    def maximumNonAdjacentSum(self, arr):
        # Get size of array
        n = len(arr)

        # If array has one element
        if n == 1:
            return arr[0]

        # Initialize dp array
        dp = [0] * n

        # Base cases
        dp[0] = arr[0]
        dp[1] = max(arr[0], arr[1])

        # Iterate from 3rd element
        for i in range(2, n):
            # Pick current + dp[i-2] or skip current
            dp[i] = max(arr[i] + dp[i - 2], dp[i - 1])

        # Final result at last index
        return dp[n - 1]

class Solution {
    // Function to return maximum sum of non-adjacent elements
    maximumNonAdjacentSum(arr) {
        // Get the size of the array
        const n = arr.length;

        // If only one element, return it
        if (n === 1) return arr[0];

        // Initialize dp array
        const dp = new Array(n).fill(0);

        // Base cases
        dp[0] = arr[0];
        dp[1] = Math.max(arr[0], arr[1]);

        // Iterate from 3rd element
        for (let i = 2; i < n; i++) {
            // Take current + dp[i-2] or just dp[i-1]
            dp[i] = Math.max(arr[i] + dp[i - 2], dp[i - 1]);
        }

        // Final result at last index
        return dp[n - 1];
    }
}

// Driver code
const arr = [2, 1, 4, 9];
const sol = new Solution();
console.log(sol.maximumNonAdjacentSum(arr));
Complexity Analysis Time Complexity: O(N), every element of array is processed once.
Space Complexity: O(N), extra space used to store DP array.
Space Optimization
Algorithm
In the tabulation approach, we stored the best possible answer up to every index in an array. But if we look closely, to compute the value at any position, we only need the answers from the previous two positions and not the entire array. Therefore, instead of storing the entire array, we can just keep track of the best possible sum at previous index and one before that. For every index:
  • If we include the current element, we add it to the best sum from two positions back.
  • If we don’t include it, we just take the last best sum from the previous position.
  • Then we update our tracking values to reflect the new best sum for the previous two positions.
Code
1234567891011121314151617181920212223242526272829303132333435363738394041
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;

class Solution {
public:
    // Function to return maximum sum of non-adjacent elements
    int maxSum(vector<int>& nums) {
        // Handle edge case for empty input
        if (nums.empty()) return 0;

        // Initialize two tracking sums
        int prev2 = 0;
        int prev = nums[0];

        // Traverse through the array
        for (int i = 1; i < nums.size(); i++) {
            // Include current by adding it to two steps back
            int include = nums[i] + prev2;

            // Exclude current by taking previous best
            int exclude = prev;

            // Choose max of include and exclude
            int curr = max(include, exclude);

            // Update tracking variables
            prev2 = prev;
            prev = curr;
        }

        // Final result is stored in prev
        return prev;
    }
};

int main() {
    vector<int> arr = {3, 2, 5, 10, 7};
    Solution obj;
    cout << obj.maxSum(arr) << endl;
    return 0;
}

    // Function to return maximum sum of non-adjacent elements
    public int maxSum(int[] nums) {
        // Handle edge case for empty input
        if (nums.length == 0) return 0;

        // Initialize two tracking sums
        int prev2 = 0;
        int prev = nums[0];

        // Traverse through the array
        for (int i = 1; i < nums.length; i++) {
            // Include current by adding it to two steps back
            int include = nums[i] + prev2;

            // Exclude current by taking previous best
            int exclude = prev;

            // Choose max of include and exclude
            int curr = Math.max(include, exclude);

            // Update tracking variables
            prev2 = prev;
            prev = curr;
        }

        // Final result is stored in prev
        return prev;
    }
}

public class Main {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        int[] arr = {3, 2, 5, 10, 7};
        Solution obj = new Solution();
        System.out.println(obj.maxSum(arr));
    }
}

            exclude = prev

            # Update tracking variables
            prev2 = prev
            prev = curr

        return prev

class Solution {
    // Function to return maximum sum of non-adjacent elements
    maxSum(nums) {
        // Handle edge case for empty input
        if (nums.length === 0) return 0;

        // Initialize two tracking sums
        let prev2 = 0;
        let prev = nums[0];

        // Traverse through the array
        for (let i = 1; i < nums.length; i++) {
            // Include current by adding it to two steps back
            let include = nums[i] + prev2;

            // Exclude current by taking previous best
            let exclude = prev;

            // Choose max of include and exclude
            let curr = Math.max(include, exclude);

            // Update tracking variables
            prev2 = prev;
            prev = curr;
        }

        // Final result is stored in prev
        return prev;
    }
}

// Driver code

console.log(obj.maxSum(arr));
